definition: mass fraction of dissolved salt material in seawater

definition_ml.en: mass fraction of dissolved salt material in seawater

source_document: IOC, SCOR and IAPSO, 2010: The international thermodynamic equation of seawater – 2010: Calculation and use of thermodynamic properties. Intergovernmental Oceanographic Commission, Manuals and Guides No.56, UNESCO
note: The Reference Composition Salinity S_R} can be calculated from the salinity S in PSU (Practical Salinity Unit) using the dimensionless unit Practical Salinity Scale (PSS) as S_R} =c·S with c = (35,16504/35) g/kg. For seawater of Reference Composition, S_R}   gives the best current estimate of S_A} . If composition anomalies are present, S_A} can be calculated from the seawater density or estimated from a global mapping of the Absolute Salinity Anomaly dS = S_A} - S_R} .

note_ml.en: The Reference Composition Salinity S_R} can be calculated from the salinity S in PSU (Practical Salinity Unit) using the dimensionless unit Practical Salinity Scale (PSS) as S_R} =c·S with c = (35,16504/35) g/kg. For seawater of Reference Composition, S_R}   gives the best current estimate of S_A} . If composition anomalies are present, S_A} can be calculated from the seawater density or estimated from a global mapping of the Absolute Salinity Anomaly dS = S_A} - S_R} .
